repository.description: The legacy user interface for OpenMRS Platform 2.x is chiefly comprised of administrative functions and the patient dashboard. commit.message:
Migrate all tests from JUnit 4 to JUnit 5/6 (#715)

* Migrate all tests from JUnit 4 to JUnit 5/6

This comprehensive migration updates 214 test files to be compatible
with openmrs-core 3.0.0-SNAPSHOT which removed JUnit 4 support.

Changes include:
- Replace all JUnit 4 imports with JUnit 5 equivalents
  (org.junit.Test -> org.junit.jupiter.api.Test, etc.)
- Convert @Before/@After to @BeforeEach/@AfterEach
- Convert @BeforeClass to @BeforeAll
- Convert @Ignore to @Disabled
- Migrate Assert.xxx() to Assertions.xxx()
- Migrate assertThat to org.hamcrest.MatcherAssert.assertThat
- Convert all @Test(expected=X.class) to assertThrows()
- Convert ExpectedException @Rule to assertThrows() with message assertions
- Rewrite OpenmrsProfileRule from JUnit 4 TestRule to JUnit 5 ExecutionCondition
- Fix message-first assertion argument ordering (JUnit 4 message-first
  to JUnit 5 message-last)
- Update base class imports from org.openmrs.web.test.BaseModuleWebContextSensitiveTest
  to org.openmrs.web.test.jupiter.BaseModuleWebContextSensitiveTest
- Update BaseContextMockTest to jupiter package
- Add junit-bom:6.0.3 to POM dependencyManagement
- Update maven-surefire-plugin to 3.5.5

All 1760 tests pass with 0 failures and 0 errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove \${revisionNumber} from omod finalName to fix CI checksum step

The shared CI workflow (build-backend-module.yml) uses
mvn help:evaluate -Dexpression=project.build.finalName to determine the
omod filename, but help:evaluate does not execute lifecycle phases. The
buildnumber-maven-plugin sets \${revisionNumber} during validate, so it
resolves to null in help:evaluate, causing the "No such file" error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

guide: make WSL cross-compile UEFI example fully copy-pasteable (#2914)

The cross-compile working example uses `--uefi` without specifying the
firmware path, which fails when WSLENV isn't configured — the Windows
binary receives a Linux-format path and can't find the firmware.

Make the default example fully copy-pasteable by adding explicit
`--uefi-firmware` with `wslpath -w` translation. Move the shorter
WSLENV-based command into a tip admonish as an alternative.

Addresses feedback from @jakeatmicrosoft on #2901.

commit.message:
Update to .NET 10, XHarness 11.0, Xcode 26.2, and CI overhaul (#93)

## Framework & Tooling

- Target .NET 10 (multi-targeting net9.0 + net10.0)
- Update XHarness to 11.0.0-prerelease.26107.1
- Update Xcode to 26.2, Android API 36
- Pin MAUI workloads to 10.0.20 (net10) / 9.0.120 (net9)
- Update runners: windows-2025, macos-15-intel, ubuntu-24.04

## NuGet Package Updates

- Spectre.Console.Cli 0.49.1 → 0.53.1 (breaking: Execute requires CancellationToken)
- Spectre.Console.Testing 0.49.1 → 0.54.0 (breaking: CommandAppTester removed)
- Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk → 18.3.0
- coverlet.collector → 8.0.0, xunit.runner.visualstudio → 3.1.5
- NUnit → 4.5.1, NUnit.Analyzers → 4.12.0, NUnit3TestAdapter → 6.1.0
- NSubstitute → 5.3.0, CommunityToolkit.Mvvm → 8.4.0
- Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.* → 10.0.3

## CI Architecture Overhaul

### GitHub Actions
- Single ci.yml with matrix strategies for build (3 configs) and test (2 OS)
- 10 composite actions for device tests under .github/workflows/test-*/
- Pack job with CI versioning (0.999-ci.$RUN_ID) for PR validation
- Separate release.yml for NuGet publishing (security isolation)
- NuGet Trusted Publishing via OIDC (NuGet/login@v1) — no long-lived API keys

### Azure Pipelines
- Single azure-pipelines.yml with inlined build/test/pack (matrix strategy)
- 10 job templates under .azure/templates/test-*.yml

### CI Improvements
- Removed all timeout-minutes / timeoutInMinutes overrides
- Removed --cores, --partition-size, --timeout from emulator boot
- Parameterized --response-threshold for emulator stability
- Added validation guards for .app/.msix file searches (fail-fast on empty)
- Search only artifacts/ for build outputs (removed hardcoded sample paths)
- validate-arch composite action on all non-Windows device test jobs

## Submodule Removal

- Removed AndroidSdk.Tools submodule → NuGet androidsdk.tool
- Removed AppleDev.Tools submodule → NuGet appledev.tools
- Cleaned up .gitmodules, .git/config, .git/modules/

## Source Code Changes

- Added CancellationToken parameter to all 14 CLI command Execute methods
- Created local CommandAppTester shim (Spectre.Console.Testing 0.54 removed it)
- Fixed TestConsole width for JSON output tests (Profile.Width = 10000)
- Multi-targeted DeviceRunners.Cli for net9.0 + net10.0

## Documentation

- Rewrote docs/ci-pipeline.md to reflect actual architecture

* Cache Docker image by content hash to skip redundant builds (#342)

* Initial plan

* Add content-hash based Docker image caching to skip redundant builds

Compute a SHA-256 hash of all files that affect the Docker image (Dockerfile,
requirements.txt, src/, web/, plugins/, tests/, nginx.conf, supervisord configs,
entrypoint.sh). Use actions/cache to store/restore a compressed image tarball
keyed by this hash.

When the cache hits, the image is loaded directly from the tarball instead of
rebuilding from scratch - completely skipping Docker Buildx setup and the
multi-stage build. Both ci.yml (e2e-tests) and integration-tests.yml share the
same cache key format so cross-workflow cache reuse is possible on the same
branch.

* Fix 3 Bugbot issues: trailing empty line drop, unused maxLines option, draft lineCount init

Bug 1 (HIGH): serializeTemplateSimple now always pushes the final line,
even when empty and beyond maxLines. Previously the condition
`if (currentLine || lines.length < maxLines)` silently dropped a
trailing empty line when the user pressed Enter past line 6, causing
the line counter and validation warning to not appear.

Bug 2 (LOW): Removed the unused LineNavigationOptions interface and
maxLines option from the LineNavigation extension. The option was
defined but never referenced after the refactor to always insert
hardBreaks. Also removed the .configure({ maxLines }) call from
TipTapTemplateEditor and tests.

immediately when a restored draft exceeds the board line limit,
rather than only appearing after the user's first edit.

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix rich text editor Enter, Backspace, and Delete key behavior

The root cause of the broken Enter key was twofold:
1. next/dynamic was serving cached JS chunks, so none of our editor
   fixes were ever reaching the browser. Replaced with a direct import
   (TipTap's useEditor({ immediatelyRender: false }) handles SSR).
2. ProseMirror's baseKeymap splitBlock command could create multiple
   paragraphs, breaking our single-paragraph document model.

Fixes applied:
- Add SingleParagraphDoc extension restricting the doc schema to
  exactly one paragraph, preventing splitBlock from ever succeeding
- Handle Enter directly in editorProps.handleKeyDown via ProseMirror's
  view.dispatch for maximum reliability
- Make Backspace and Delete "see through" invisible ZWS cursor-anchor

* chore: bump version to 2.1.54 [skip ci]

* Input delay investigation (#347)

* perf: reduce live edit input-to-display latency from ~550ms to ~120ms

Frontend changes:
- Add live edit fast path: single 100ms debounce + single API call
  (replaces double-debounce 150ms+200ms + double API call chain)
- Use immediate state (not debounced) for live path responsiveness
- AbortController cancels stale in-flight requests during rapid typing
- Skip redundant preview API call when live mode is active
- Skip 100ms visual transition delay during live editing

Backend changes:
- Change render/live endpoint from async to sync def to prevent
  blocking FastAPI event loop with synchronous requests.post()

The old pipeline: keystroke → 150ms debounce → 200ms debounce →
renderTemplate API → 100ms transition → renderTemplateLive API → board

The new pipeline: keystroke → 100ms debounce → renderTemplateLive API →
preview + board update (single call)

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: fix stale display updates — force fresh renders + reduce polling default

Root cause: The background polling loop called preview_page() WITHOUT
force_refresh, so it used cached previews up to 5 minutes old. Even
with 60s polling, template variables (weather, time, stocks) would
only update every ~5 minutes — matching the reported behavior.

Fixes:
- Force fresh renders in background polling loop (bypass preview cache)
- Reduce preview cache TTL from 300s to 120s for non-polling usage
- Reduce default polling interval from 60s to 30s for new installs
  (existing users keep their saved setting; configurable down to 10s)

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: abort in-flight live request when content cleared or on cleanup

Abort liveAbortRef in both the !hasContent early return and the effect
cleanup function to prevent stale API responses from overwriting the
null preview state after the user clears all content.

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Change default polling interval from 30s to 15s

Reduces the default polling interval to 15 seconds across backend and
frontend for more responsive board updates:

- Backend PollingSettings default: 30s -> 15s
- Frontend status/plugin refetchInterval: 30000ms -> 15000ms
- Updated tests to reflect new default

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix frontend polling interval default to match backend (15s)

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CI test failures

- Fix spacecraft_launches manifest repository URL capitalization
  (Fiestaboard -> FiestaBoard)
- Fix flaky silence toggle test: wait for useDeferredValue to settle
  by checking polling interval initialization before interacting
- Remove temp TESTING_SPACECRAFT_LAUNCHES.md per workspace rules

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: cancel in-flight preview mutation when enabling live mode

When liveOutputEnabled becomes true, the auto-preview effect now cancels
any in-flight previewMutation by setting shouldIgnoreNextResponse and
calling previewMutation.reset(). It also clears needsRePreview to prevent
stale mutation chains. This prevents the normal mutation's onSuccess from
racing with the live fast path and overwriting the preview with stale data.

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove duplicate closing bracket in renderTemplateLive (rebase artifact)

The rebase conflict resolution left a stray '}),' after the
renderTemplateLive function definition, causing a parse error that
broke both the Next.js build (E2E) and Vitest (UI tests).

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix package-lock.json version mismatch: update from 2.1.49 to 2.1.51 to match package.json

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: replace undefined applyAlignment with processLinesWithPrefixes in live fast path

The live edit fast path called applyAlignment(), which was never defined
or imported, causing a silent ReferenceError. Combined with the normal
preview effect returning early when liveOutputEnabled is true, this meant
live mode produced no preview updates and no board updates at all.

Replace the undefined call with processLinesWithPrefixes (used by every
other code path) and pass the resulting cleanedLines + metadata to
renderTemplateLive.

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add duopyramid polytopes: {6}+{6}, {8}+{8}, {10}+{10}, {12}+{12}

Dual of {2n}x{2n} duoprisms, with vertices on S3 as two regular
polygons in orthogonal planes. Rings traced via reflection method
(all lie on great circles). Hopf fibration bundles used for coloring.

Key analysis results documented in spec.md:
- Rank formula: m+n if m or n odd, m+n-1 if both even (proved)
- God's number: m+n if full rank, (m+n)/2 otherwise (verified by BFS)
- Move distribution follows binomial coefficients C(m+n, k)
- Hopf bundles: n+1 bundles with sizes (n, n, ..., n, 2)
- Reference tables for symmetric and asymmetric cases

Support Attention(24)-CUDA and disjoint from contrib op (#27542)

Fix #27485
Reference https://github.com/microsoft/onnxruntime/pull/27486

## Summary

Implements CUDA kernel support for the **ONNX Attention op (opset 23 and
24)** using a thin-dispatcher architecture that directly calls Flash
Attention, Memory Efficient Attention (cutlass FMHA), and Unfused
(GEMM+softmax+GEMM) kernels — bypassing the contrib MHA/GQA dispatch
layers entirely. This covers both Multi-Head Attention (MHA) and
Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) variants, with support for internal KV
cache (past/present), external KV cache (`nonpad_kv_seqlen`), boolean
and float attention masks, combined `nonpad_kv_seqlen + attn_mask`
composition, and 3D (BSNH) / 4D (BNSH) input formats.

The kernel respects `ORT_DISABLE_FLASH_ATTENTION` and
`ORT_DISABLE_MEMORY_EFFICIENT_ATTENTION` environment variables,
consistent with contrib MHA/GQA behavior.

---

## Architecture: Thin-Dispatcher Design

The core kernel (`attention.cc`) implements a **3-path dispatch
cascade** with clear priority:

```
ComputeInternal
  │
  ├─ Early reject: softcap, softmax_precision (not supported by any CUDA kernel)
  │
  ├─ Flash Attention    (fp16/bf16, Ampere+, head_size==v_head_size, no output_qk)
  │   ├─ Path 1: nonpad_kv_seqlen (opset 24 external cache) → mha_fwd_kvcache
  │   ├─ Path 2: past_key + past_value (internal cache decode) → mha_fwd_kvcache
  │   │           Supports: bool mask → seqlens_k, no mask → fill past_seq_len
  │   └─ Path 3: no past, no mask (prompt) → mha_fwd
  │   Excluded: nonpad + mask combo (no bias param), float masks on prompt
  │
  ├─ Memory Efficient   (fp16/bf16, SM75+, no past_key, bias stride aligned)
  │   ├─ Path 1: nonpad_kv_seqlen (±mask) → custom_right_padding + additive bias
  │   ├─ Path 2: with mask (prompt) → standard MEA with additive bias
  │   └─ Path 3: no mask (prompt) → standard MEA
  │
  └─ Unfused Fallback   (all dtypes, all mask types, MHA only)
      ├─ nonpad_kv_seqlen (±mask) → attention_bias with additive composition
      └─ Bridges to contrib MHA's QkvToContext (GEMM+softmax+GEMM)
```

GQA head expansion is handled via `LaunchUngroup` in the MEA path. Flash
handles GQA natively via `kv_num_heads` parameter.

---

## What DOESN'T Work ❌

| Combination | Reason | TODO? |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **GQA + fp32** (any) | `MEA LaunchUngroup` is fp16/bf16 only; unfused
rejects GQA | ✅ TODO added |
| **GQA + float mask + decode** | Flash rejects float masks; MEA rejects
decode; unfused rejects GQA | Known limitation |
| **Softcap on CUDA** | All 3 kernels reject | ✅ TODO added |
| **softmax_precision on CUDA** | All 3 kernels reject | ✅ TODO added |
| **output_qk** beyond `kNone`/`kQK` | Only unfused supports, and only
`kNone`/`kQK` | ✅ TODO added |

\* 3 kernels mean Flash/MEA/Unfused

### Additional Known Limitations

| Limitation | Details |
| :--- | :--- |
| **attn_mask shorter than total_seq** | Spec allows padding with -inf;
validation enforces exact match. Tracked separately. |
| **No past_present_share_buffer** | Decode always copies past→present
(memset + strided copy). Contrib GQA can share buffers for zero-copy. |
| **No XQA kernel** | Contrib GQA's specialized decode kernel requires
shared buffers. ONNX Attention's internal-cache decode is ~15-30% slower
for GQA workloads. This does not apply when using TensorScatter +
`nonpad_kv_seqlen` (opset 24), which avoids the copy overhead entirely.
|

---

## Bug Fixes

- **2D mask shape interpretation (MEA path):** Fixed incorrect
interpretation of 2D `attn_mask` as `[B, kv_seq]` — the ONNX spec
defines it as `[q_seq, total_seq]`. The old code could read
out-of-bounds when `batch_size > q_seq` (e.g., batched decode). Fix uses
MEA's native broadcast flags (`strideB=0, strideH=0`) instead of an
expansion kernel, which is both correct and more efficient.

- **4D BNSH present_key/value (prompt path):** Added D2D
`cudaMemcpyAsync` for the `!is_bsnh` case in both Flash and MEA prompt
paths. Previously, `present_key`/`present_value` outputs were silently
left uninitialized for 4D BNSH inputs. Decode path already worked via
`mha_fwd_kvcache`; unfused already worked via `QkvToContext`.

- **Cutlass FMHA bias alignment:** Added alignment checks for attention
bias strides in the FMHA launch template, mirroring checks in the kernel
to prevent runtime enforcement errors on misaligned strides.

- **SEGFAULT fix** (`group_query_attention_impl.cu`): Added missing
explicit template instantiations for `LaunchUngroup<__half>` and
`LaunchUngroup<__nv_bfloat16>`. The new `attention.cc` calls
`LaunchUngroup` cross-TU, but without explicit instantiations the symbol
was undefined at runtime → `dlopen` crash → all CUDA test binaries
failed.

- **`val >= 0` assert fix** (`attention_mask_impl.cu`): Fixed overly
strict `val > 0` asserts in Flash kernel and AttentionBias kernel —
`val=0` is valid for count semantics. Kept `val > 0` in GQA kernel with
comment explaining last-valid-index convention.

- **Negative seqlens_k from all-false mask** (`attention_mask_impl.cu`):
When mask is all-false (`seq_len=0`) and decode offset is negative,
`seq_len + seqlen_offset` produced hugely negative `seqlens_k` passed to
flash. Fixed with `max(0, seq_len + seqlen_offset)` clamp.

- **BFloat16 Flash gate fix:** Fixed `disable_flash_attention_`
constructor gate that used `!std::is_same<T, MLFloat16>::value`,
accidentally excluding BFloat16 from Flash. Changed to `sizeof(T) != 2`
to match contrib GQA/PackedMHA/PagedAttention pattern.

---

## Changes by Area

### Attention Operator and Kernel Registration
* Added support for the Attention operator in opset 24 for CUDA,
including kernel registration for `float`, `MLFloat16`, and `BFloat16`
types, and refactored opset 23 kernel registration to use versioned
kernel macros for clarity and future extensibility.

### Env Var and Kernel Options Support
* Added `disable_flash_attention_` and
`disable_memory_efficient_attention_` members read from
`AttentionKernelOptions` via `GetAttentionKernelOptions()`. The ONNX
Attention kernel now respects `ORT_DISABLE_FLASH_ATTENTION` and
`ORT_DISABLE_MEMORY_EFFICIENT_ATTENTION` environment variables, matching
the pattern used by all contrib CUDA attention ops.

### Tensor Transposition Utilities
* Replaced 9 copy-paste `if constexpr` type-switch blocks with 2
templated helpers (`TransposeBNSHtoBSNH<T>`, `TransposeBSNHtoBNSH<T>`)
for efficiently transposing tensors between BxNxSxH and BxSxNxH formats
for `float`, `half`, and `BFloat16` types. Net -117 lines.

### Attention Mask and Sequence Length Handling
* Enhanced the mask-to-sequence length conversion kernel to support both
GQA and Flash conventions with a configurable offset, improved handling
for all-false masks, and refactored the launch function to accept the
offset parameter.

### nonpad_kv_seqlen + attn_mask Composition
* MEA path: supports both via `has_custom_right_padding` (seqlens_k) +
additive attn_bias simultaneously.
* Unfused path: converts nonpad to attention_bias, then composes mask
additively via `LaunchAddBiasInPlace`. Supports 2D `[q, t]` and 4D `[B,
1, q, t]` mask shapes.
* Flash path: excluded (no bias parameter when seqlens_k is used).
Warning logged on fallback.

### Alignment Checks for Attention Bias
* Added stricter alignment checks for attention bias strides in the
CUTLASS FMHA launch template, mirroring checks in the kernel to prevent
runtime enforcement errors.

### Helper Functions and API Improvements
* Added a new helper function for ungrouping in group query attention
(`LaunchUngroup`) and improved the output shape computation for
attention to optionally skip nonpad data validation for GPU paths.
* Refactored the CUDA Attention kernel class to split logic into
dedicated methods for FlashAttention, MemoryEfficientAttention, and
UnfusedAttention, each with ASCII dispatch diagrams documenting
supported parameter combinations.

---

## Testing

**39 C++ tests** in `attention_op_test.cc` covering opset 23/24
correctness, nonpad+mask composition (bool and float), BFloat16 Flash
guard, all-false mask regression, batch≠q_seq broadcast guard, and
nonpad edge cases.

New test files under
`onnxruntime/test/python/transformers/test_onnx_attention/`:

- **`test_mha.py`** (14 test classes, 16 test functions) — MHA path:
flash, MEA, unfused (fp32), BF16, 3D/4D inputs, bool/float masks,
past/present KV cache, causal mode, broadcast masks. Unfused tests
explicitly disable flash+MEA via env vars.
- **`test_gqa.py`** (10 test classes, 13 test functions) — GQA path:
flash, flash BF16, MEA, 3D/4D inputs, head ratios (32:8, 8:2),
bool/float masks, past/present KV cache, 4D BNSH present_kv correctness,
nonpad_kv_seqlen (Flash + MEA + CPU).
- **`test_tensorscatter_attention.py`** (5 test classes) — External KV
cache with `nonpad_kv_seqlen` (opset 24), TensorScatter-based cache
management, fp16 + fp32, with-mask variants (bool + float).
- **`common.py`** — Shared test infrastructure: ONNX model builders,
reference implementations, config classes.

commit.message:
Fix OCR agent crash after read_binary_file / when sending to LLM (#141)

* Initial plan

* fix: OCR agent crash after readBinary / when sending base64 to LLM

Two root causes fixed:

1. examples/agents/ocr-agent.md — system prompt now correctly describes
   the automatic ContentFilePart workflow; removed the outdated instruction
   to manually embed base64 text in the LLM message. The previous prompt
   caused the LLM to expect raw base64 but receive a ContentFilePart,
   breaking the OCR workflow after read_binary_file.

2. src/tools/predefined.ts — added a 50 MB file-size guard in
   ReadBinaryFileTool before attempting base64 conversion. Encoding a very
   large file requires multiple in-memory copies (ArrayBuffer + binary
   string + base64 string + JSON request body), which could exhaust the
   Electron renderer heap and crash the app.

Tests: 5 new integration tests cover normal read, file-not-found, the
50 MB size limit (both over and just-under), and shouldRequireHITL.

Co-authored-by: merlinbecker <3381241+merlinbecker@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: enforce platform-aware file size limits for mobile Capacitor compatibility

- Use Obsidian's Platform.isMobile to select a tighter 20 MB limit on
  Capacitor (iOS/Android) vs. 50 MB on Electron desktop. base64 conversion
  requires ~4x the file size in RAM; mobile devices have far smaller memory
  budgets and would OOM-crash on large files that desktop handles fine.
- btoa, chunked String.fromCharCode and ArrayBuffer are all standard Web
  APIs available in both Electron and Capacitor WebViews — no other
  compatibility issues found.
- Add Platform mock to tests/mocks/obsidian.ts.
- Add mobile-specific rejection and just-under-limit acceptance tests;
  introduce setPlatformMobile() helper for cleaner test code.

Co-authored-by: merlinbecker <3381241+merlinbecker@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
consensus: Fix sync loop blocking catch-up production

When all peers are at our height but we're far behind target, the
sync function was falling through to a 120s sync loop that found
nothing. Now: request fresh chain tips, wait 3s, re-check. If still
no peer ahead, return Ok() to allow catch-up block production.
